sections.paragraph:
ἀπογραφή, ῆς, ἡ (s. ἀπογράφω; Lysias, Pla. et al.; SIG 1023, 45 and 71; 1109, 34; 1157, 33, OGI 338, 11 and 34; very freq. pap; LXX, EpArist, Joseph.; Just., A I, 34, 2, D. 78, 4) administrative term ‘list, inventory’ of the statistical reports and declarations of citizens for the purpose of completing the tax lists and family registers (s. Mitt-Wilck. I/1 175f; 178; 202ff; 225ff, I/2 198ff, esp. 202, the census edict of C. Vibius Maximus, 104 a.d. [=PLond III, 904, 25f, restored]; on this Dssm., LO 231f [LAE 268f]). Lk 2:2 the word means census, registration, of the census taken by Quirinius. Joseph. puts a census taken by Q. in 6/7 a.d. (cp. Jos., Bell. 7, 253, Ant. 18, 3). Presumably Ac 5:37 ἐν τ. ἡμέραις τ. ἀπογραφῆς also refers to this census. The chronology is full of problems, on which see the handbooks. See Schürer I 399–427 (lit.); Ramsay, Bearing 238ff; Zahn, Lk 129–35 and Exk. IV; EKlostermann, Hdb. on Lk 2:1–3; M-JLagrange, RB n.s. 8, 1911, 60–84; EGroag, Prosopogr. Beitr. VII (JÖAI 21/22, 1924 Beiblatt, cols. 445–78); HWindisch, NThT 16, 1927, 106–24; AvPremerstein, Ztschr. d. Savigny-Stiftg. f. Rechtsgeschichte 48, 1928, Rom. Abt. 449ff; LRTaylor, AJP 54, ’33, 120–33; RSyme, The Roman Revolution ’39, 397–401; ESeraphin, CBQ 7, ’45, 91–96; FHauck, Theol. Hndkomm., Lk p. 37; Goodsp., Probs. 71f; MHombert-CPréaux, Recherches sur le recensement dans l’Egypte romaine, ’52; EStauffer, Jesus, Gestalt u. Geschichte, ’57, Die Dauer des Census Augusti: Studien zum NT u. zur Patristik, ’61, 9–34; HInstinsky, D. Jahr der Geburt Christi, ’57; HBraunert, Historia 6, ’57, 192–214, Cives Romani und ΚΑΤʼ ΟΙΚΙΑΝ ΑΠΟΓΡΑΦΑΙ: Antidoron MDavid in Papyrologica Lugd.-Bat. vol. 17, ’68, 11–21 (lit.; EStauffer, Festschr. Klostermann ’61, 9ff; JThorley, The Nativity Census: What Does Luke Actually Say?: Greece and Rome, ser. 2, 26, ’79, 81–84; Haenchen ad loc.; BPalme, ProBib 2, ’93, 1–24). S. also on ἡγεμονεύω and Κυρήνιος.—Boffo, Iscrizioni 182f (lit.). New Docs 6, 115–19. DELG s.v. γράφω. M-M.

Lysias Lysias , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Mitt-Wilck. Mitt-Wilck. = LMitteis/UWilcken, Grundzüge und Chrestomathie der Papyruskunde—Lists 4, 6

esp. esp. = especially

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

Dssm. Dssm. = ADeissmann, various works—List 6

LO LO = Licht vom Osten—s. Dssm., LO in List 6

LAE LAE , s. Dssm. LO—List 6

Q Q = Qumran, cited with numbers indicating cave of origin—List 2, end

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Ramsay, Bearing Ramsay, Bearing = The Bearing of Recent Discovery on the Trustworthiness of the NT

Zahn Zahn = TZ., various works—List 6

Hdb. Hdb. = Handbuch (in connection with a specific NT book, the ref. is to a commentary in the series Handbuch zum Neuen Testament, founded by HLietzmann)—List 6

RB RB = Revue Biblique—List 6

n.s. n.s. = new series (also used to render Neue Folge)

NThT NThT = Nieuw Theologisch Tijdschrift—List 6

AJP AJP = American Journal of Philology—List 6

CBQ CBQ = Catholic Biblical Quarterly—List 6

Goodsp., Probs. Goodsp., Probs. = Problems of NT Translation—List 6

NT NT = New Testament

Bat. Bat. = PBatiffol, s. JosAs—List 5

vol. vol. = volume

Festschr. Festschr. = Festschrift, in ref. to any honorary publication

ser. ser. = series

Haenchen Haenchen = EH., Commentary on Acts—List 6

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

ProBib ProBib = Protokolle zur Bibel—List 6

Boffo, Iscrizioni Boffo, Iscrizioni = LB., Iscrizioni greche e latine—List 3

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 108
